purpose: Issue presigned object upload instructions and validate upload policy inputs for large file workflows without proxying file bytes through the application.

endpoints.description: Generate presigned upload instructions for one object using caller-approved storage settings.

dependencies.purpose: Represent S3-compatible upload commands and request parameters

agent_usage.when_to_use: Use when the application should let clients upload directly to object storage with short-lived credentials.
agent_usage.when_not_to_use: Do not use for permanent credentials, upload byte proxying, or unrestricted bucket-wide write access.
agent_usage.example_workflow: 1) Validate caller ownership and object key policy. 2) Call /uploads/presigned. 3) Client uploads directly to storage using returned method, url, and headers.

failure_modes.condition: Requested object key or expiry violates platform policy
failure_modes.recovery: Use an allowed object key prefix and expiration window

allowed_hosts.purpose: Generate and validate S3-compatible request shapes

forbidden_behaviors.description: Must not write outside declared scope
Must not grant upload access outside approved object key scope

agent_notes.implementation_hint: Use S3Client with getSignedUrl and PutObjectCommand. Enforce a maximum expiration and approved key prefix policy before signing. Return only the minimum headers required by the signed request.
agent_notes.known_gotchas: Presigned URLs inherit the permissions of the signing credentials. Expiry windows that are too long widen the blast radius of leaked URLs.
agent_notes.preferred_approach: Treat signing as a narrow boundary module with explicit bucket and key policy checks before URL generation.
agent_notes.avoid_approach: Do not proxy upload bytes through the API and do not return raw cloud credentials.
